title: Mimikatz Signature Detected
description: Windows Defender detected memory patterns consistent with Mimikatz lsass.exe credential dumping tool on WORKSTATION01. Process: powershell.exe (PID 4412). Parent: cmd.exe.

title: Pass-the-Hash Detected
description: Logon type 3 (network) authentication using NTLM from WORKSTATION01 to DC01 with a hash that does not match any recently rotated password. Possible pass-the-hash attack. Account: CORP\svc_backup.

title: Admin Share Access
description: Successful connection to \\DC01\ADMIN$ from WORKSTATION01 (10.10.5.42) using svc_backup credentials. SMB admin share access from non-admin workstation is anomalous.

title: WMI Remote Execution
description: WMI CreateProcess call from 10.10.5.42 (WORKSTATION01) targeting FILESERVER02 (10.10.3.20). Command line: 'cmd.exe /c whoami && ipconfig /all > C:\Windows\Temp\out.txt'. Executed as SYSTEM.

title: Admin Share Access
description: Successful connection to \\FILESERVER02\C$ from 10.10.5.42. File enumeration activity detected: 47 directory listing calls within 30 seconds. Consistent with automated reconnaissance.

title: Service Installation via SCM
description: New service 'WinHttpSvc' installed on FILESERVER02 via Service Control Manager from remote host 10.10.5.42. Binary path: C:\Windows\Temp\svc.exe. This is a common persistence mechanism.

title: Pass-the-Hash Detected
description: NTLM pass-the-hash from FILESERVER02 (10.10.3.20) targeting DC01 domain controller (10.10.1.5). Account: CORP\Administrator. Possible token relay from previously installed service.

title: Mimikatz Signature Detected
description: Mimikatz lsass dump detected on DC01 (10.10.1.5). Attacker has reached the domain controller. Credential extraction from NTDS.dit likely imminent. Process: svc.exe (PID 1288) running as SYSTEM.
